coreJobStatement: Cultivate finfish from egg or fingerling to market-size harvest in a controlled aquatic environment
productJobStatement: Help Flow Measurement to quantify water circulation rate across rearing and treatment loops in recirculating aquaculture systems without obstructing flow or clogging from biofilm
lPath: L7 Finfish Aquaculture Ecosystem > L6.10 Water Quality Monitoring & Control > L5.10.1 Flow Measurement > L4.10.1.1 Ultrasonic Flow Sensor

segments.name: Large-scale commercial RAS (>500 t/yr, salmon focus)
segments.characteristics: $50M+ capex, 50–200 rearing tanks, full SCADA, 24h staff, biosecurity certification
segments.targetabilityReason: demands mag meters or industrial ultrasonic; zero tolerance for measurement gaps
segments.circumstance: $50M+ capex, 50–200 rearing tanks, full SCADA, 24h staff, biosecurity certification
segments.alternativesDiffer: Yes — demands mag meters or industrial ultrasonic; zero tolerance for measurement gaps

segments.name: Mid-scale RAS farm (50–500 t/yr, tilapia/trout/barramundi)
segments.characteristics: $2–15M capex, 10–50 tanks, semi-automated PLC, part-time night coverage
segments.targetabilityReason: cost-sensitive but high mortality risk; open to ultrasonic upgrade from paddle-wheel
segments.circumstance: $2–15M capex, 10–50 tanks, semi-automated PLC, part-time night coverage
segments.alternativesDiffer: Yes — cost-sensitive but high mortality risk; open to ultrasonic upgrade from paddle-wheel

segments.name: Hatchery / fingerling producer
segments.characteristics: Juvenile stock worth $0.50–5.00/fry, extremely sensitive to DO and temperature swings, small pipe diameters (DN15–DN25)
segments.targetabilityReason: precision required but budgets tight; paddle-wheel or no measurement common
segments.circumstance: Juvenile stock worth $0.50–5.00/fry, extremely sensitive to DO and temperature swings, small pipe diameters (DN15–DN25)
segments.alternativesDiffer: Yes — precision required but budgets tight; paddle-wheel or no measurement common

segments.name: Flow-through / raceway operator
segments.characteristics: Continuous water exchange, no recirculation loop, high flow volumes, gravity-fed typical
segments.targetabilityReason: flow monitoring optional (water replaces itself); sensor value is intake metering
segments.circumstance: Continuous water exchange, no recirculation loop, high flow volumes, gravity-fed typical
segments.alternativesDiffer: Yes — flow monitoring optional (water replaces itself); sensor value is intake metering

segments.name: Emerging / startup RAS (<50 t/yr, first-generation facility)
segments.characteristics: Limited operations expertise, capital-constrained, often university spin-out or investor-backed
segments.targetabilityReason: needs lowest total cost of ownership; paddle-wheel or manual observation dominant
segments.circumstance: Limited operations expertise, capital-constrained, often university spin-out or investor-backed
segments.alternativesDiffer: Yes — needs lowest total cost of ownership; paddle-wheel or manual observation dominant

alternatives.name: Ultrasonic transit-time flow sensor
alternatives.tradeoffs: ±1–3% accuracy (vs ±0.5% mag); degrades with air bubbles >2% vol; requires 5–10×DN straight pipe run; needs fluid-type configuration at commissioning; emerging awareness in aquaculture [SRC: web_search]

alternatives.name: Electromagnetic (mag) flowmeter
alternatives.tradeoffs: 5–10× cost ($500–2,000+ vs $100–300 for inline ultrasonic); higher accuracy (±0.5%) but overkill for most farm-scale monitoring; larger footprint; higher power consumption (10–20 W); requires conductive liquid (met by default in freshwater RAS) [SRC: web_search]

alternatives.name: Paddle-wheel / turbine flow sensor
alternatives.tradeoffs: Very low cost ($20–80); BUT clogs with biofilm in 2–6 weeks in RAS water; moving parts wear in particulate-laden water; contamination risk from bearing lubricant; no anomaly detection; ±2–5% accuracy drift; 45–55% incumbent share [SRC: VN_finfish_farming, web_search]

alternatives.name: Differential-pressure measurement (orifice/venturi)
alternatives.tradeoffs: Permanent pressure drop wastes pump energy (critical in energy-intensive RAS); fouling of sensing ports by biofilm; limited turndown ratio (3:1); requires DP transmitter; rarely used in aquaculture [SRC: web_search]

alternatives.name: Do nothing / manual observation [Status Quo]
alternatives.tradeoffs: Zero cost — 15–25% of farms; completely blind to flow failure; relies on downstream DO alarm or fish behavioral change; by the time operators notice, mortality may already be underway; historically acceptable on small ponds, catastrophic in RAS [SRC: VN_finfish_farming]

jobSteps.description: the required water flow rate per rearing tank and treatment loop based on stocking density, species, and water treatment capacity
jobSteps.sensorDependencyRationale: Sensor range must match the required flow window; over/under-specification has biological consequences
jobSteps.rawStatement: Define the required water flow rate per rearing tank and treatment loop based on stocking density, species, and water treatment capacity
jobSteps.rationale: Sensor range must match the required flow window; over/under-specification has biological consequences

jobSteps.description: Install, commission, and configure the flow sensor for the specific water conditions (freshwater, brackish, or recirculated)
jobSteps.sensorDependencyRationale: Commissioning procedure and medium configuration are sensor-dependent
jobSteps.rawStatement: Install, commission, and configure the flow sensor for the specific water conditions (freshwater, brackish, or recirculated)
jobSteps.rationale: Commissioning procedure and medium configuration are sensor-dependent

jobSteps.description: Verify the sensor is reading within specified accuracy before stocking fish or after system maintenance
jobSteps.sensorDependencyRationale: Pre-stocking verification is critical; flow sensor failure after stocking = mortality risk
jobSteps.rawStatement: Verify the sensor is reading within specified accuracy before stocking fish or after system maintenance
jobSteps.rationale: Pre-stocking verification is critical; flow sensor failure after stocking = mortality risk

jobSteps.description: Measure water circulation rate continuously across all rearing tanks and treatment stages 24/7/365
jobSteps.sensorDependencyRationale: Core measurement function — the primary value-creating and LIFE-SAFETY action in RAS
jobSteps.rawStatement: Measure water circulation rate continuously across all rearing tanks and treatment stages 24/7/365
jobSteps.rationale: Core measurement function — the primary value-creating and LIFE-SAFETY action in RAS

jobSteps.description: flow readings for drift, anomalies, pump degradation, or pipe blockage; trigger alarms on flow failure
jobSteps.sensorDependencyRationale: Flow failure alarm is the most critical safety trigger in RAS — DO crash follows within minutes
jobSteps.rawStatement: Monitor flow readings for drift, anomalies, pump degradation, or pipe blockage; trigger alarms on flow failure
jobSteps.rationale: Flow failure alarm is the most critical safety trigger in RAS — DO crash follows within minutes

jobSteps.description: Adjust water distribution, redirect flow, or troubleshoot when flow readings deviate from expected values
jobSteps.sensorDependencyRationale: Sensor data quality determines troubleshooting speed in a life-threatening situation
jobSteps.rawStatement: Adjust water distribution, redirect flow, or troubleshoot when flow readings deviate from expected values
jobSteps.rationale: Sensor data quality determines troubleshooting speed in a life-threatening situation
